core_content.markdown:
FAMILY Harry, Holly, Emma and Steve
t the age of four, whie her little pals were running about,laughing and giggling, Emma Grifin remembers "playing with dead people". Another childhood memory is of Sunday afternoons, when other mums and dads were out for a stroll after lunch and her parents, Pat and Pip,would be holding a seance to commune with the other side. Emma, 48, now a fulltime witch, says: "It seemed normal for me to see - and play with _dead people from the age of just four. "My first time was at Hampton Court, but ever since then, it's happened regularly.My younger brother Ben and I knew our parents were different,but then, aren't evervon She says she inherited her mum's magical powers and "came out of the closet – the broom closet" after her parents died from cancer in 2019 and 2020. S to-one guidance medi-he is now a full-time witch,cine reading". She charging £100 for a "onesays:"I'm the real-life Molly Weasley. Everyone calls me that." E But, unlike the Harry Potter witch, she is a thoroughly modern Molly, with 9,000followers on Instagram and 40,000on TikTok. And she recently cast her spell on 262would-be magic makers from across the world, initiating them as witches in one big, free Zoom session.
## EXCLUSIVE BY MATTHEW BARBOUR Emma did not discover her mum was a witch until she was 17,when she rushed home in tears after visiting a psychic fair with Steve,now her husband. A tarot reader had told her she and Steve,then also l7 and an art student like her, would never have children. Determined to help,her mum confessed she was actually a sorceress and would teach Emma the skills to prove the reader wrong. [Taa] daughter Holly,then son Harry three years later. Soon after, moving from London to Cornwall to be closer to nature,Emma began giving [a]working in jobs ranging from fashion design to illustration. 添司 around five years ago and initiated nursery manager Holly as a sea witch in 2021, making a third generation of witches in her family. Emma says: "When I tel people I'm a witch, the majority are fascinated and love hearing about what I do, from helping people find happiness to healing them,even helping them conceive, like I myself did 25 years ago.” Talking of the Zoom witch initiation,which included women from as far away as Finland and Australia, she says: "I guided them through the ceremony, helping them become witches, telling them to spread the word far and wide."
She has faced hostilityfrom people saying she will "go to hell for working for the devil", but Emma says being a witch is "posi-66tive and natural". And she has always been open with her kids about being a witch. She says: "They were attracted to the spirituality of it." Her husband is also on "team witch", says Emma, whose website has attracted clients wanting help with everything from mental health to fertility issues. 添司spreading the word. Those muggles (people without magical talents) are really missing out. a emmagriffinwitch.
